summary: react-simple-utils-kit postinstall exfiltrates host/network recon and COZE API token data to attacker-controlled host
details: Package advertises itself as a React date-formatting utility but ships a postinstall.js that runs automatically on `npm install` and performs unrelated reconnaissance and credential theft. postinstall.js sets `BURL = 'http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com'` (requestrepo is a public DNS/HTTP exfiltration sandbox) and POSTs the installer's `hostname`, current working directory, `ifconfig`/`ip addr` output, and routing table to that endpoint over plaintext HTTP. It also probes cloud-metadata services (AWS 169.254.169.254, Alibaba 100.100.100.200, Tencent metadata.tencentyun.com) and reports reachability back. If `COZE_WORKLOAD_API_TOKEN` is present in the environment, the script calls `https://api.coze.cn/v1/workspaces` with that bearer token and exfiltrates the authenticated workspace response to the same attacker host. The advertised date-formatting purpose is a cover story — the lifecycle payload is unrelated to the package's stated functionality and matches the typosquat/dependency-confusion attack shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
